Document:
# Novakin
Novakin maintain a nitrogen reserve and a fuel-fed core. Nitrogen always leaks into the local atmosphere; a full intact reserve lasts ten minutes. Nitrogen inhalers refill it; other gases do not.
Below 50% reserve, nitrogen loss causes ordinary blood-loss damage that worsens as the reserve falls. They have no bloodstream: drink medicine normally, while injected medicine only works through a cryotube, which delivers it to the Core for digestion. Fuel keeps the core warm; an empty fuel reserve cools rapidly.
Temperatures below 323.15 K (50 C) or above 700 K damage the shell until medical critical. This cannot kill directly, but a shattered shell leaks its full reserve in 30 seconds until the Novakin is stable and back in a safe temperature. In vacuum, space harm begins after a 60-second grace period and resets after returning to pressure.
Novakin are 15% more vulnerable to blunt, slash, and piercing damage. Their glowing eyes are always visible, and their night vision only enhances the 120 degree arc ahead of them.
# Survival equipment
